description: The cosmic ocean of milk that was churned by gods and demons to obtain the nectar of immortality (amrita). Vishnu rests on the serpent Shesha floating on this ocean.
name: Hindu Cosmology

keyMyths.title: Samudra Manthan (Churning of the Ocean)
keyMyths.description: Gods and demons collaborate to churn the cosmic ocean of milk, seeking amrita (the nectar of immortality). This event releases both beneficial and harmful substances, ultimately shaping the world and the roles of deities.

keyMyths.title: The Cosmic Egg (Brahmanda)
keyMyths.description: The universe originates from a golden egg (Hiranyagarbha) that emerges from the primordial waters. Brahma, the creator, hatches from this egg and begins the process of creation within its shell.

keyMyths.title: The Avataras of Vishnu
keyMyths.description: Vishnu descends to Earth in various forms (avataras) to restore cosmic order and dharma when it is threatened. These incarnations, such as Rama and Krishna, are crucial for maintaining balance in the universe.

keyMyths.title: The Destruction and Re-creation of the Universe (Pralaya)
keyMyths.description: The universe undergoes cyclical periods of creation, preservation, and destruction (pralaya). At the end of each cycle, Shiva destroys the universe, paving the way for Brahma to create anew.

keyMyths.title: The Story of Markandeya
keyMyths.description: The sage Markandeya is granted immortality by Shiva after facing Yama, the god of death, demonstrating the power of devotion and the transcendence of cosmic cycles. He witnesses a partial destruction of the universe during his trials.

sources.name: The Rigveda
sources.description: The oldest of the Vedas, containing hymns and philosophical insights that form the basis of Hindu cosmology.
sources.title: The Rigveda

sources.name: The Upanishads
sources.description: A collection of philosophical texts that explore the nature of reality, the self (Atman), and the ultimate reality (Brahman).
sources.title: The Upanishads

sources.name: The Puranas
sources.description: A vast collection of encyclopedic texts that contain myths, legends, genealogies, and cosmological accounts.
sources.title: The Puranas

sources.name: Stella Kramrisch, The Presence of Siva
sources.description: An academic study of the deity Shiva and his role within Hindu cosmology and philosophy.
sources.title: Stella Kramrisch, The Presence of Siva

sources.name: Heinrich Zimmer, Myths and Symbols in Indian Art and Civilization
sources.description: An academic exploration of the symbolic representations and mythological narratives within Indian art and culture, including cosmological themes.
sources.title: Heinrich Zimmer, Myths and Symbols in Indian Art and Civilization
